evidence_quotes.quote: In mid2025, thousands of adultthemed games were removed or deindexed from Steam and itch.io due to pressure from Mastercard (and Visa) after advocacy from an Australian group, Collective Shout

Mastercard, a longtime sponsor of Riot Games esports, reportedly requested that Riot moderate or remove negative comments about its role during livestream chats of events like the Valorant Champions Tour.
Viewers discussing the censorship have been silenced or timed out.

paragraphs.text: As a concerned EU consumer, Im writing to express serious worries about emerging financial censorship by major payment processors and its impact on digital fairness. Issue 1 - Mastercards Influence Over Game Distribution In mid2025, thousands of adultthemed games were removed or deindexed from Steam and itch.io due to pressure from Mastercard (and Visa) after advocacy from an Australian group, Collective Shout (https://www.wired.com/story/gamers-are-furious-about-the-censorship-of-nsfw-games-and-theyre-fighting-back/) (https://www.theguardian.com/games/2025/jul/29/why-did-adult-titles-disappear-from-steam-itch-pc-gaming-payment-processors) Developers, including LGBTQ+ and kinkfriendly creators, have condemned this as unfair suppression, financial institutions, not laws, are effectively dictating what content remains accessible. Issue 2 - Suppression of Speech During Riot Games Broadcasts Mastercard, a longtime sponsor of Riot Games esports, reportedly requested that Riot moderate or remove negative comments about its role during livestream chats of events like the Valorant Champions Tour. Viewers discussing the censorship have been silenced or timed out. (https://www.si.com/esports/news/riot-games-blocking-mastercard-discourse-in-vct-chat) These practices strike at the heart of digital fairness: - Consumers shouldnt be deprived of legal, creative content due to opaque moral judgments by payment providers. - E-sports viewers must be allowed to engage in open discussion and critical opinions about sponsors should not be suppressed. I urge the EU to: - Recognize and address financial censorship under unfair commercial practices, particularly where intermediaries unduly restrict lawful digital content. - Strengthen safeguards against corporate overreach that bypasses democratic and legal accountability. - Protect freedom of expression in digital platforms, including gaming communities and livestream forums. Ensuring a level playing field and upholding digital rights means preventing private entities from imposing moral gatekeeping via financial control. That aligns directly with the goals of the REFIT initiative. Thank you for considering this critical consumer concern.
